steps.title: Find the key signal — what should data describe
steps.content: Liu et al. concluded that these alloys' toughness performance differs from most HEAs and MEAs. To support this conclusion, we need data that clearly describe how their toughness behaves—the actual performance pattern across conditions. The question asks which choice best describes data supporting that conclusion.

steps.title: Match the data to the choices
steps.content:
Choice D identifies the clearest pattern shown throughout the graph: for both alloys, both types of toughness are substantially lower at 293 K (room temperature) than at 20 K (cryogenic). CrCoNi initiation drops from ≈500 to ≈300; propagation from ≈550 to ≈400. CrMnFeCoNi initiation drops from ≈400 to ≈250; propagation from ≈450 to ≈300. This describes a comprehensive, consistent pattern across all measurements.

Choice A only describes data at one temperature point. Choice B's claim that propagation stays “substantially higher” than initiation throughout all temperatures is contradicted at 77 K, where they converge. Choice C claims changes are greater between 20 and 77 K than between 77 and 198 K, but for CrCoNi initiation, the drop from 77 to 198 K (≈150) exceeds the drop from 20 to 77 K (≈50), directly contradicting this.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
